folio.label: 51v
folio.l: And groped here and there , and she fond non
Allas q¶ she , I had almost mysgou~
I had almost gon , to the Clerkes bedde
Ey bn~dicite , than had I foule spedde
And fortú she goth , til she the cradil fonde
She gropeth alwey , ferthere with hire honde
And fond the bed , and æou©t nat but good
By cause that the cradil , by it stod
And nyst where she was , for it was derke
And faire & wele , she crepte in by the clerke
And lith ful stille , and wold han caugút a slepe
W Inne a while , this Ioún the Clerk vp lepe
And on this goode wyf , he leyd on sore
So mery a fit , ne had she nat ful ©ore
He pryketh hard , and depe , as he were mad
This ioly lyf , han theise two Clerkes lad
Til æat the .iij. Cok , bygan to syngö
¢ Aleyn wex wery , in the morwenyngö
For he had swonken , al the longö nygút
And seide , fare wel Malkyn , swete wygút
The day is come , I may no lengere byde
But euäe more , where so I go or ryde
I is thyn owen Clerk , so haue I seele
¢ Now dere le~man q¶ she , go fare wele
But er thow go , o thyngö I wyl the telle
Whan that æè wendest homward , by the Mille
Rigút at the entre , of the dore behynde
Thow shalt a cake , of a busshel fynde
That was I maked , of thyn owen Mele
Whicú æat I halpe , my sire for to stele
And good lemman , god the saue & kepe
And with that word , almost he gan to wepe
¢ Aleyn vp ryst , and thougút er æ it dawe
I wyl go crepe in , be my felawe
And fond the cradil , at his hand a non
By god q¶ he , al wrangö I had mysgon
Myn hed is toty , of my swynk to nygút
That makes me , that I go nat aa rigút
I wat wel by the Cradel , I haue mysgaa
He lyes the Millere , and his wyf alswa
¢ And forth he gotú , a twenty deuele way
Vn to the bedde , æäe as the Millere lay
He wend han cropen , by his felawe Ioún
And by the Millere , In he crepe a non
And caugút hi~ by æe nekke , and softe he spake
